question_text: Earth's Moon orbits at a distance of approximately 384,000 km and takes 27.3 days to complete one orbit. If a hypothetical moon orbited Earth at half this distance, how long would its orbital period be? Show your working.
explanation: Using Kepler's Third Law: T₁²/T₂² = r₁³/r₂³. If r₂ = 0.5 × r₁, then T₂² = T₁² × (0.5)³ = T₁² × 0.125, so T₂ = T₁ × √0.125 ≈ T₁ × 0.354. Therefore T₂ ≈ 27.3 × 0.354 ≈ 9.7 days. This shows that closer orbits have much shorter periods because the moon experiences stronger gravitational pull and travels a shorter path.
